steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Text Structure and Purpose question. The decisive clue is the sentence “It reminded me of when I was little”: it explicitly links what Darius is experiencing in the car (Farsi drifting from the front seat) to a childhood memory (his mother’s bedtime chants). The question asks for the main purpose of the passage as a whole.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — To name some of the narrator's favorite poets who wrote in Farsi.: Rumi and Hafez appear only as incidental details in the mother’s chanting; the passage never evaluates or ranks them as favorites, and naming poets is not the text’s purpose. ✗
  • Choice B — To describe a situation the narrator thinks is annoying.: The tone is nostalgic and soothing (“quiet and soothing”), not annoyed; nothing in the passage signals irritation or frustration. ✗
  • Choice C — To reveal that the narrator's mother is a poet.: The mother recites poems by Rumi and Hafez, meaning she chants the work of others — the passage never suggests she is herself a poet. ✗
  • Choice D — To connect a current experience to a memory from the narrator's past.: The entire passage moves from Darius dozing in the car surrounded by Farsi directly into the childhood memory of his mother’s bedtime chanting — that movement is the passage’s structure and purpose. ✓
That matches Choice D — To connect a current experience to a memory from the narrator's past..
